text: Every producer brings into being things like to itself before things unlike itself.
All that proceeds from any principle remains in that principle, proceeds from it, and reverts upon it.
The Good is that to which all things tend, and which all desire — that which is the source and end of all striving.
The One is the measure of all things; in proportion as each being participates in unity, it exists.
Time is the moving image of eternity, proceeding according to number from the eternal order of the intelligible world.
The soul illuminates the body as the sun illuminates the world — by presence, not by mixing; by shining, not by descending.

Every multiplicity participates in some way in unity. For if it did not participate in unity in some way, it would neither be a whole nor consist of parts — for each of the parts must be one part, and the whole must be one whole.
All things aspire to the Good as to their proper perfection, and from the Good they receive the power to accomplish what is natural to them.
